Doubling the middle letter makes it mean 'first' as a description instead of just the action of being first
Adding ين makes it refer to many people or things that are first
Adding ال at the start means we're talking about specific first ones, not just any first ones

steps.m: the first ones (object of إِلَّآ أَسٰطِيرُ)
steps.n: The word 'first ones' is the object being talked about in 'tales of the first ones'
This is a time word that shows something happened in the past. In this ayah, it introduces when the disbelievers said something.

steps.m: and when (introducing what the disbelievers said)
steps.n: The و (wa) at the beginning connects this story to the previous stories about disbelievers
Added fatha marks to make the past tense form. The و changes to ا because it's between two vowels.
Adding وا at the end shows that a group of people did the action
The root letters for 'deity' get a special change to become Allah, the name of the One God
The notes are incorrect - adding 'u' at the end does not make it 'O Allah'. The word still means 'Allah' but the 'u' shows Allah is the subject/doer in the sentence
Adding م with shadda at the end changes it to a special way of calling out to Allah
Adding the fatha at the end completes the special vocative form for calling upon Allah
This is a conditional particle that introduces a possibility. In this ayah, it starts a condition about 'if this is the truth', which is followed by their request for punishment.
Added fathas to make it past tense. This is the theoretical form before the weak letter change.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic for this type of word.
Basic demonstrative element meaning 'here' or 'this'
Added ذا to make it specifically mean 'this' for masculine singular things
Added alif with overbar (ٰ) and fatha marks to show proper pronunciation
This is a personal pronoun that refers back to هٰذَا (this). It's the subject of كَانَ (was) in the phrase 'if this was the truth'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
The doubled letter shows emphasis and makes it a noun meaning the concept itself
Adding definite article makes the noun definite
The fatha shows this word is affected by the verb كَانَ (was) in this ayah
This is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere. In this ayah, it connects to the word عِندِكَ (Your presence), showing that the truth would be coming from Allah's presence.

steps.m: to be with or near something/someone

steps.n: Added vowels to make it into a word showing location or possession (like saying 'at the place of' or 'in possession of')

steps.m: from your presence/possession (مِنْ عِندِكَ = from you)
steps.n: The كَ shows this belongs to or is with 'you' (when speaking to one male). This whole phrase مِنْ عِندِكَ means 'from you' in a more formal or emphatic way.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added hamza at beginning to make the verb causative - showing someone made it rain rather than it raining naturally
Changed vowels and added sukun to make it a command
Added فَ to show this is a result or consequence of something before it
This is a preposition showing something is on top of or over something else
The ى changes to يْ when adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added نَا at the end which means 'us', showing who is being affected by the action in قَالُوا (they said)
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic noun
Changes vowels and adds letters to make it mean 'many stones' instead of 'one stone'

steps.m: stones (as direct object of فَأَمْطِرْ/rain down)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this is the direct object of the verb 'rain down' in the phrase 'rain down upon us stones'
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'
The م gets a shadda because it comes after the word هُوَ which ends in a vowel
The ن gets a fatha and connects to the next word السَّمَآءِ (the sky), making it mean 'from the sky'
Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high up
Added ٱل (the) at the beginning. The س doubles (gets shadda) because it's a sun letter
The word 'sky' gets a different ending (-i sound) because it comes after مِنَ (meaning 'from'). The middle letter is also written slightly differently but pronounced the same.
This is a basic conjunction word that gives a choice between options
Added kasra at the end because it connects to the next word ٱئْتِنَا (bring us). This helps the words flow together smoothly when reading.
Connected the root letters together
Changed the first letter to hamza with sukun and added kasra on ت to make it a command. The ي is dropped in command form.
Added نَا at the end to show the command is directed at 'us' as the recipients (come to us/bring to us)
Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun that describes the result of the action.
Adding بِ at the start shows this is being used with/by something
Adding tanween (double kasra) makes the word indefinite (a/an)
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this describes something that causes intense pain. This word describes عَذَابٍ (punishment) in this ayah.
The tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this is indefinite (like 'a/an' in English) and follows the word it describes (عَذَابٍ)
This is a basic particle that can mean either 'what' (for questions) or 'not' (for negation)
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before. In this ayah, it's used with كَانَ to say 'and was not'
Added fathas to make past tense. This word shows who is doing the action in يُعَذِّبَ (to punish) in this ayah.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to say.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns

Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال with a shadda, replacing the initial hamza with alif-wasla, and adding the nominative case ending. This word is unique and specifically refers to the one true God in Islam.
Adding shadda (ّ) doubles the middle letter ذ to show the punishment is strong or happens multiple times
Adding يُ at the start changes it from 'to punish' to 'he punishes' - showing Allah is doing the action

steps.m: in order that he punishes (subject is اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added لِ at the start to show this is explaining why or for what purpose Allah would do something

steps.m: in order that he punishes them (subject is اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who receives the punishment (them)
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: and you (speaking to one male) (who is present while Allah's messenger ﷺ is among them)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This is addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, confirming his presence among the people.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment

steps.m: in them (referring to those who Allah would punish)
steps.n: Adding هِمْ shows this applies to a group of males being talked about. In this ayah, it refers to those who Allah would punish.
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation of this connecting word
Added ما which here means 'not' because it's used with كان to negate a past action. This combination (وما كان) means 'and was not' or 'and would not'.
This is a basic verb about existence or occurring
Added vowel marks (fathas) to change from basic root meaning 'to be' into 'he was' in the past tense
Added alif after first letter to make the past tense verb 'he was'. This is a common change for hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in middle)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns

Added ال to make it definite (the God), doubled the ل with shadda, and added ُ to show this is the subject of the verb كَانَ (was). The hamza from إلَه is dropped and replaced with ٱ when ال is added. This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam.
This root relates to punishment or suffering
Added shadda to show strong/intensive punishment
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'one who' and changed vowels to show it's the doer of the punishing
Added fatha at the end because it's the object (receiver) of كَانَ (was)

steps.m: going to punish them (object of كَانَ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who is being punished
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation of the connecting particle
Added هُمْ which is a pronoun meaning 'they/them' (masculine plural). This word is the subject of يَسْتَغْفِرُونَ (they seek forgiveness) in this ayah.
This root is about forgiveness and covering up faults
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or requesting forgiveness
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is a particle that can mean either 'what' (question word) or 'not' (negation). Here it's used with كَانُوٓا۟ to negate it, meaning 'they were not'.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This و means 'and'.
Added fatha to show the vowel sound

steps.m: for them (connected to يُعَذِّبَهُمُ - punish them)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who the action is for. This word connects to يُعَذِّبَهُمُ (punish them) in the ayah, showing who might be punished.
Starting with the word أَنْ which means 'that' - this connects what comes before with what comes after
Added لَا (not) after أَنْ to negate what comes after
The ن of أَنْ combines with the ل of لَا, creating a shadda (ّ). This is just a pronunciation change, not a meaning change.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows the punishment is stronger or happens multiple times
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ

steps.m: he is punishing them severely (subject of اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added هُمُ at the end to show who is being punished. This word is what Allah (اللَّهُ) is doing in this ayah.
This is a unique word that became the proper name for The One True God in Islam. The ال is part of the word itself, not just added like normal 'the'.

steps.m: Allah (as the doer of يُعَذِّبَ - punish)
steps.n: Added the ُ to show Allah is the one doing the action of punishing in this verse. The ّ (shadda) shows emphasis on the L sound. The hamza at the start is written as ٱ when connecting to previous words.
Basic pronoun meaning 'they' (referring to the disbelievers who are preventing others from the Sacred Mosque)
Added conjunction و (and) to connect this clause with what came before. This word is the subject of يَصُدُّونَ (they prevent/block)
The doubled middle letter shows this is done with force and persistence
Changed from past to present by adding يَ at start. The middle letter now has damma (ُ) sound.

steps.m: they prevent/block (referring to the disbelievers preventing access to the Sacred Mosque)
steps.n: Made plural to show multiple disbelievers doing this action together. This relates to وَهُمْ (and they) earlier in the verse.
Basic preposition showing separation or moving away from something
Added kasra at the end to connect it with the next word الْمَسْجِدِ (the mosque). This shows that people are being prevented away from the mosque specifically.
Added مَ at start to show 'place where' something happens, like how a mosque is where people pray
Adding ال makes it definite (the). The ِ ending shows it connects to the next word الْحَرَامِ (the sacred), together meaning 'the Sacred Mosque'

steps.m: to prevent access, to make sacred/protected

steps.n: The added alif makes it a noun describing something that is permanently forbidden
Adding ال makes it definite, and the ِ ending shows it's in the genitive case
This is a basic particle that can mean either 'what' (for questions) or 'not' (for negation)

steps.m: and what/and not (connecting to يَصُدُّونَ - they prevent)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word to the previous idea. Here it's used with يَصُدُّونَ (they prevent) to ask 'and what right do they have' or state 'and they have no right'
Added vowels to connect the letters and make it past tense
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)
Added plural masculine ending وا to show multiple people did the action

steps.m: to be close to and protect

steps.n: Added vowels and doubled the last letter to create the basic noun form meaning 'one who protects'
Changed from single protector/friend to plural form (like going from guardian to guardians)
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon, and extended the alif for emphasis

steps.m: his protectors/close friends (acted upon by كانوا)
steps.n: Added 'his' at the end. This word is affected by كانوا (they were) in the ayah

steps.m: if (used with كَانُوٓا۟ in this ayah)
steps.n: Basic conditional particle that shows something depends on something else. Here it's used with كَانُوٓا۟ (they were) to talk about a past condition.

steps.m: to be close to and protect

steps.n: Adding vowels changes this into a noun meaning someone who protects
Changed to plural form using the pattern أَفْعِلَاء which is used for important roles or qualities
Added ُ to show this is the doer in the sentence. The hamza on waw (ؤ) is just a spelling rule for hamza after long vowels.

steps.m: its protectors/allies (referring to the Sacred Mosque)
steps.n: Added هُ to show these are the protectors of the Sacred Mosque mentioned earlier. The ۥٓ marks are just pronunciation helpers.

steps.m: except (connects ٱلْمُتَّقُونَ as an exception to أَوْلِيَآءَهُ)
steps.n: This is a particle used for exception. It connects two parts of speech by showing that the second part (ٱلْمُتَّقُونَ - the God-conscious people) is an exception to the first part (أَوْلِيَآءَهُ - His allies).

steps.m: to guard against harm or evil

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added مُتَ at the beginning and changed the ending to make it mean 'someone who guards themselves' (reflexive)
The و is replaced by ت which then combines with the existing ت to form تّ
Added ٱلْ to make it specific
Added ُونَ to show this refers to multiple people
Added alif and kasra to show the basic form of the word
Added shadda and fatha at the end to make the word stronger and more emphatic
Added و at the beginning to connect this contrasting statement with what came before it
The vowels َُ show this is about becoming more of something
Added أ at beginning and changed vowels to make comparative form
Added هُمْ at the end to show it refers to 'them'
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَعْلَمُونَ (they know) into 'they do not know'. It's like adding 'not' before a verb in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense (he knew) to present tense (he knows) by adding يَ at the start

steps.m: they know (referring to most people in لٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ)
steps.n: Added masculine plural ending ون to show multiple people. This refers back to 'most of them' (أَكْثَرَهُمْ) in the verse.

steps.m: what/not (can ask questions or make negatives)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that can either ask questions (like 'what?') or make something negative (like 'not/no')

steps.m: and what/not (connects to صَلَاتُهُمْ - their prayer)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what comes after it. Here it's connecting to 'their prayer' (صَلَاتُهُمْ) in the ayah
Added fathas to make past tense verb meaning 'he was'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb
Root letters connected with short vowels (fat-ha) to make basic word for prayer
Changed و to ا and added ة to make it a noun for a single instance of prayer
Added ُ to show this is the subject of كان (was)
Added هُمْ to show the prayer belongs to them
Adding kasra to first letter changes this from an action into a word showing location
Adding fatha at the end helps connect this word to what comes after it

steps.m: to house, to shelter, to provide lodging

steps.n: Basic noun pattern with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter. This word is the object of عِندَ (near/at) in this ayah, showing where their prayer was taking place.
Adding kasra shows this word is connected to what comes before it (عِندَ)

steps.m: the house (referring specifically to the Ka'bah)
steps.n: Adding definite article prefix. In this context, البيت refers specifically to the Ka'bah (the sacred house in Makkah).

steps.m: except (what comes after this word is excluded from what was mentioned before)
steps.n: This is a special word that shows exception - it tells us that what comes after it (مُكَآءً وَتَصْدِيَةً - whistling and clapping) is different from what was mentioned before (their prayer). It's like saying 'Their prayer was nothing... except whistling and clapping.'

steps.m: to make sounds with your mouth (like whistling)

steps.n: Changed the word to show the action itself instead of doing the action
Changed spelling of alif to show it's a long sound
Added -an ending to show this is receiving the action (like saying 'they made a whistling')

steps.m: to clap or make noise with hands

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed to a noun showing the action itself
Added tanween to show this is the object being acted upon
Added 'and' to connect this to the previous word مُكَآءً (whistling). Both are objects of كَانَ (was) in this ayah.
Changed into a command by adding damma (u sound) to the start - like saying 'you taste!'
Added وا to make the command apply to multiple people
Added فَ to show this is a result of what came before - they were mocking prayer, so as a result, they are commanded to taste the punishment
Connected the root letters with fathas to make it a past tense verb
Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun that describes the result of the action
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific punishment rather than punishment in general
The fatha at the end shows this is what is being tasted/experienced in response to the command 'taste!' (ذُوقُوا۟)
Starting with the basic word ما which means 'what'
Added بِ at the beginning to show cause or reason. This word connects to تَكْفُرُونَ (you disbelieve) to mean 'because of what you disbelieve'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to damma and sukun to form the basic command form. The middle و is dropped because it's a weak letter.
Added تُ to show past tense for first person (I) or second person (you)
Added مْ to make the verb plural, showing it's addressing multiple people
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at the start to change from 'he' to 'you', and changed the vowels to show present tense

steps.m: you all reject/deny (referring to the disbelievers at the Ka'bah)
steps.n: Added plural ending ون to show multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to the disbelievers who were mocking prayer at the Ka'bah.
This is the basic form of the word that can mean 'if' or 'whether'
Adding the shadda changes this from a conditional word to one that emphasizes the truth of what follows. In this ayah, it emphasizes the statement about those who disbelieve.
Added ال with a shadda to make it specific. This word helps connect descriptions to people or things.

steps.m: those who (subject of كَفَرُوا - they disbelieved)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word introduces the people who disbelieved.

steps.m: to reject, deny, or cover up truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: they rejected/denied (truth) (subject of يُحْشَرُونَ - they will be gathered)
steps.n: Adding وا changes the subject from 'he' to 'they (a group of men or mixed group)'. This word is the subject of يُحْشَرُونَ (they will be gathered) in this ayah.
The small circle (۟) is added above وا to show it's a plural marker. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he spent) to present tense (he spends) by adding يُ at the start. The middle letter gets a different vowel mark.

steps.m: they spend (money/resources) (subject of يَصُدُّوا - to prevent)
steps.n: The ون suffix makes this plural, referring to the disbelievers (ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا) who are spending their wealth to prevent others from Allah's path
Root letters م-و-ل became مَال by changing و to ا (alif) to make the basic noun for 'wealth'
Changed from single to plural by adding أ at start and وا in middle - like changing 'money' to 'monies'
Changed alif to alif maksura and added fatha to show this is the object of يُنفِقُونَ (they spend)

steps.m: their wealth/properties (object of يُنفِقُونَ - they spend)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show the wealth belongs to them
Adding vowel marks creates the simplest past tense verb form
Added يَ at start to make it present tense, doubled the middle letter with ّ (shaddah), and changed middle vowel to ُ
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: so that they may block/prevent (from سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose - they do this in order to block others from Allah's path
This is a preposition showing movement or turning away from something. In this ayah, it shows people are being turned away from سَبِيلِ (the path).
Added basic vowel marks to connect the root letters
Added ي to make the common noun meaning 'path'
Added kasra at the end to show this is possessed by Allah (اللَّهِ) in the phrase 'path of Allah'
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the hamza was dropped because this is a special divine name

Added kasra at the end to show possession, doubled the ل with shadda, and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word. This word is possessed by سَبِيلِ (path) in the phrase 'path of Allah'.
Changes root into 'he' form of present tense (adds yu- prefix)
Future tense marker س added before present tense verb to indicate future action
Changed to plural form to show multiple people will be doing the spending. This matches with الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (those who disbelieved) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added فَ to show this is a consequence or result of what was mentioned before (their disbelief)

steps.m: and they will spend it (acted on)
steps.n: Added feminine singular object pronoun ها referring back to أَمْوٰلَهُمْ (their wealth) mentioned earlier in the verse. The pronoun shows what will be spent.
This is a basic particle that shows something happens after something else

steps.m: then (connects to يُغْلَبُونَ - they will be overcome)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) and shadda (ّ) to show this word strongly connects what happened before (they spend their wealth) with what happens after (they will be overcome)
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he/it existed'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels

steps.m: it becomes (subject will be حَسْرَةً - regret)
steps.n: Changed to present tense with تَ prefix. The و returns because it's not between two vowels anymore. In this ayah, this verb tells what will happen to their wealth - it will become a source of regret.
Basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to ي when adding a suffix
Added هِمْ at the end to show who is being acted upon (them)

steps.m: to feel deep regret or loss

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun that describes the feeling itself by adding ة and changing the middle vowel to sukun

steps.m: as a source of deep regret (describing أَمْوٰلَهُمْ - their wealth)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this describes how their wealth will become a source of regret for them

This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. Here it connects 'they spend' (يُنفِقُونَ) with 'it becomes' (تَكُونُ), showing that the money they spend will later become a source of regret.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
The يُ prefix shows someone is receiving the action instead of doing it
Added plural ending ون to indicate multiple subjects
Added ال to make it definite and shadda on ذ to strengthen the meaning
Added ين at the end to make it plural, referring to multiple people

steps.m: and those who (connected to previous statement)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added fathas to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject/doer
The fatha (َ) on first letter shows this is past tense active voice
Basic particle showing direction or movement toward something
Added alif khanjariya (small alif) at the end to show this word connects to جَهَنَّمَ (Hell) in the ayah, indicating 'to Hell'
This is the basic form of the word 'Jahannam' (Hell), which is a non-Arabic loanword that doesn't come from an Arabic root.
Added vowels and doubled the ن with shadda to show the strong, intense nature of Hell
Added fatha at the end to show this is the destination being referred to in the verse, matching with إِلَىٰ (to) that comes before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice present tense by adding يُ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, and damma at end

steps.m: they are being gathered (referring to the disbelievers mentioned in الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are being gathered. This refers back to 'those who disbelieve' (الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he separated) to present tense (he separates) by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel
Adds لِ to show purpose - this word explains why something else happens (in order to distinguish)
Changed the root into a noun for something that is worshipped
Adds the u-vowel mark at the end to show this is the subject
Makes the word specific by adding 'the' at the start
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Adding ي between second and third letters makes it into an adjective describing something with this quality
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific evil thing

steps.m: the evil/corrupt one (object of يَمِيزَ - distinguishes)
steps.n: Adding fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the verb يَمِيزَ (distinguishes)
Basic preposition that shows something coming from, being separated from, or being made from something else
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word ٱلطَّيِّبِ (the good). This shows that مِنَ is separating or distinguishing the good from something else.
Added vowels and shadda to make it an adjective describing pure or good things
Adding ال makes it definite. The first letter gets a shadda because it comes after ال

steps.m: from the pure/good one (object of مِنَ)
steps.n: The kasra ending shows this word follows مِنَ (from). The alif changes to a waṣla mark because it's connected to the previous word.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb
Adding يَ at start and changing vowels shows ongoing action. The middle letter gets sukun (ْ) to show it's not stressed.

steps.m: and he makes/sets (connected to لِيَمِيزَ - so that He may distinguish)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at start and changed final vowel to fatha (َ) because it follows لِ (so that) from earlier in the verse
Added ي between second and third root letters and vowel marks to show this describes something with a strong quality of being evil

steps.m: the evil one (object of يَمِيزَ - distinguishes)
steps.n: Added ال (the) at start and َ at end to show this is 'the evil one' receiving the action
Root meaning 'split' becomes a noun meaning 'a piece' by adding vowels
Shows this word is receiving the action of يَجْعَلَ (he makes/puts)
Adds 'his' to show ownership - the هُۥ means 'his' or 'it'
This is a preposition that comes from the basic concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition showing something being above or having authority over something else
The small alif (ٰ) is added to show this is a special form of ى that doesn't change at the end of words
The root letters ب-ع-ض get vowels added to make the noun 'part'. Like taking a whole thing and breaking off one piece of it.
Added the two dots below (ٍ) to show this part is connected to another part, like saying 'some upon some'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters

steps.m: and then he piles up (acted on)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this action follows after something else in the ayah (after يجعل)
Adding the attached pronoun suffix هُۥ to indicate the object being piled up
Added ي between second and third root letters to show the complete state of being gathered

steps.m: all together (describing how Allah will gather them)
steps.n: Added ًا (an) at the end to make it an adverb - shows HOW something happens (all together)

steps.m: to make something into something else, or to put/place something somewhere

steps.n: Added يَ at beginning to show present tense with 'he' as subject. This verb follows يَمِيزَ in the ayah, so it takes the same subjunctive form.
Added conjunction فَ to show sequence

steps.m: and then he puts it all together (referring to الْخَبِيثَ - the evil)
steps.n: Added هُۥ (it) at the end to show what is being put together - points back to 'the evil' mentioned earlier. The fatha (small line) before هُۥ connects this action to the previous verb.
This is a preposition that shows something being inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows that something will be placed in جَهَنَّمَ (Hell).

steps.m: in (something will be placed in جَهَنَّمَ)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to complete the word. This is a spelling rule for Arabic prepositions.
This is a proper noun borrowed from Hebrew 'Gehenna'. It doesn't come from an Arabic root
Added vowel marks and shadda. This word is the object of the preposition فِي (in/into) in this ayah, showing what something is being put into.
This is the base form of the demonstrative pronoun that points to things or people far away
The alif with overbar (ٰ) extends the sound to emphasize distance
Adding ئِ specifies that we're talking about people or things that can be counted
The كَ at the end adds emphasis and completes the demonstrative pronoun. This word is the subject that هُمُ (they) refers back to in this ayah.
This is the basic form of the third person plural masculine pronoun meaning 'they'
Added damma marks to show this is the subject form of the pronoun. This pronoun is connected to the word ٱلْخٰسِرُونَ (the losers) - together they mean 'they are the losers'

steps.m: to lose, be at loss, fail

steps.n: Added alif after first letter to show who does the action
Changes from one loser to many losers by adding ون at the end

steps.m: the losers (those being described as هُمُ)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite and final vowel to show its role in the sentence. This word describes who "they" (هُمُ) are in this ayah.
Command form (like 'say!') made by dropping the middle letter و and adding a sukun. Used when Allah tells Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak.
Starting with the basic demonstrative word that points to something
Adding ال and ي changes it from pointing at something to describing someone or something
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one
Adding لِ with shadda shows this is 'for' or 'to' those people. The shadda appears because لِ combines with ال. This word connects to كَفَرُوٓا۟ (they disbelieved) which comes after it.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this is the subject doing the action
The suffix وا indicates masculine plural 'they' as the subject

steps.m: if (introduces condition for يُغْفَرْ - will be forgiven)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a possible situation. Here it introduces the condition 'if they stop' which leads to the result 'they will be forgiven'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make basic past tense verb
Added اِنتَ to show someone stopping themselves (like saying 'self-stopped')
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing final vowel
Added وا at the end to show multiple people doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show that the action is being done to something rather than someone doing the action

steps.m: it is forgiven (condition in إِن يَنتَهُوا۟)
steps.n: Added يُ at the beginning to show present tense, and changed vowels to show this is a condition (if this happens). This is part of a conditional statement starting with إِن (if) in the ayah.

steps.m: for them (referring to الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا - those who disbelieved)
steps.n: Added هُم (them) to لَ (for/to) to show who the action is for. This word connects to يُغْفَرْ (will be forgiven) in the ayah to show who will receive forgiveness.
Basic particle meaning 'what' or 'that which'
The shadda emphasizes the word. In this ayah, it refers to the past actions of those who disbelieved.
This is a particle that emphasizes that something has definitely happened in the past. In this ayah, it emphasizes that the way of dealing with previous nations has already been established.

steps.m: to go ahead or pass by
it passed (referring to what happened before)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fathas to show this happened in the past. In this ayah, it refers to past actions that have already happened.
Combines و (and) with إِن (if)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The يَ at the start shows it's happening now, and the middle ُ shows it's active voice
Adds plural masculine suffix to indicate 'they'
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation. قَدْ is used to emphasize that something has definitely happened or will happen.

steps.m: then indeed (connects to يَعُودُوا۟ - they return)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before. Here it shows the result of the condition 'if they return'.
Added fathas to connect the root letters, making it past tense
Added ت to show feminine subject (she/it), and the final ي is dropped because it's a weak letter at the end when followed by ت

steps.m: to follow a path or establish a practice

steps.n: Added damma and doubled the middle letter to show this is about the practice itself, not just the action of practicing
Added ة to make it refer to one specific practice or way
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action in مَضَتْ (has passed). The ة changes to ت when adding the ُ ending.
Root letters أ-و-ل became أَوَّل by adding vowel marks and doubling the middle letter و to make it mean 'first'
Adds 'the' (ال) and 'een' ending to make it 'the first ones' (plural)

steps.m: the first ones (object of مَضَتْ سُنَّتُ)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is affected by the verb مَضَتْ (passed) in the phrase مَضَتْ سُنَّتُ (the way/practice passed)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding alif after first letter shows mutual action between two parties
Changed from past tense 'he fought' to a command 'fight!' for a group by changing the vowels
Added و at the end to show it's a command for a group of people
Added هُمْ to specify who should be fought
Added و at beginning to connect with previous statement
This is the basic form of the word, which is a particle showing a limit or endpoint
Added fatha marks and doubled the middle letter to strengthen the meaning

steps.m: until, up to (connects to لَا تَكُونَ - there is no)
steps.n: Added the alif maqsurah (ىٰ) at the end, which is part of its standard form. In this ayah, it connects to لَا تَكُونَ meaning 'until there is no...'
Basic negative particle that negates what comes after it. In this ayah, it negates تَكُونَ (there being)
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Added vowel marks (damma) to make the root letters into a complete verb meaning 'to be'
Added تَ at beginning to make it mean 'you/it' in present tense
Changed the ending vowel to fatha to make it subjunctive mood

steps.m: to test or try someone through hardship

steps.n: Root letters connected with vowels (ِ and ْ) to make a noun meaning 'test/trial' instead of the action of testing
Added ة to make it a specific instance of trial/persecution. This word is the subject of تَكُونَ (to be) in this ayah.
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is any trial/persecution, not a specific one
Changes from basic concept to 'he' doing the action in present time
The fatha at the end shows this is after 'that' (أَنْ)

steps.m: and that he/it is/exists/happens (connected to تَكُونَ)
steps.n: Adding و to connect this with تَكُونَ (there is) in the phrase 'until there is no persecution and the religion is all for Allah'

steps.m: to follow/obey a path or way

steps.n: Adding kasra and connecting the letters transforms the basic meaning into a noun for the system or way that people follow
Adding shadda emphasizes the meaning, showing it's a complete system
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific religion being discussed

steps.m: the complete way of life/religion (subject of يَكُونَ)
steps.n: The damma shows this word is the subject of يَكُونَ (to be) in this ayah
Added damma and shadda to make it mean 'all' or 'every'
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of يَكُونَ (it becomes/is)
Added هُۥ at the end to show what 'all' refers to. This whole word is the subject of يَكُونَ (it becomes/is) in this ayah.
This is the base word Allah, with shadda on ل showing emphasis
Added لِ at beginning to show purpose/direction and ِ at end to connect it grammatically. This shows that something belongs or is directed to Allah in this ayah.
Basic conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it introduces the condition of them stopping their actions.

steps.m: and if/then if (connects to وَقٰتِلُوهُمْ - and fight them)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous command. It shows this condition follows from the previous statement about fighting.

steps.m: to stop, to end, to desist

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Added reflexive pattern to show doing action to oneself
Changed to plural form by adding وا and changing final ى to و
Added helper alif to make it easier to pronounce the word
This is a basic emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like saying 'truly' or 'certainly' in English
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement about if they stop. It's like saying 'so' or 'then' in English to connect two ideas.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity'
Added fatha mark to show this word is the object of something
Added ال to make it refer specifically to The One God (Allah)
This is a basic interrogative particle meaning 'what' that can also mean 'that which' or 'whatever' depending on context. Here it means 'what' in reference to their actions (يَعْمَلُونَ).
Added the preposition بِ (with/by) to show how Allah sees or is aware of their actions. This connects to the word يَعْمَلُونَ (they do) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create past tense verb
Prefix ي indicates 3rd person masculine singular present tense

steps.m: they act/work/do (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding ون at the end, showing multiple people doing the action instead of one person
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed to the special form that shows Allah's perfect and complete attribute of seeing everything
The word is the same in both cases - the tanween dammah (double damma) is already there. The difference is just in how it's being used - first as a name for Allah, then as a general description.
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with the word تَوَلَّوْا۟ (they turn away) that comes after it
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled ل shows this is done with intensity or repeatedly
The تَ at start shows this is something done to oneself

steps.m: they turned themselves away (subject of فَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This word connects to فَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ (then know) that comes after it in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed from 'he knew' to a command 'know!' by adding alif at start and changing the middle vowel from 'i' to 'a'
Added وا to make it plural - now addressing multiple people
Added فَ to connect with previous statement (وَإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ - 'and if they turn away')

steps.m: indeed/verily (emphasizes اللَّهَ مَوْلَىٰكُمْ - Allah is your protector)
steps.n: Adding the shadda (double letter mark) makes the meaning stronger. This word connects to and emphasizes the next part of the sentence that tells us 'Allah is your protector'.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters with shadda

steps.m: Allah (as the object being known in فَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being known in the phrase 'then know that Allah...'

steps.m: to protect, support, or be close to

steps.n: Added alif symbol (ٰ) to show long vowel sound

steps.m: your (all of you) protector (referring to Allah)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this refers to all of you. This word refers to Allah being the protector of the believers.
Changes to a special verb used to praise something

steps.m: to protect, support, or be close to

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning and ى at the end to show who does the protecting. The و changes to وْ because it comes before ل.
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to point to a specific protector, not just any protector
Changes to a special verb used to praise something
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect it with what came before. This word is describing Allah as the excellent protector (ٱلْمَوْلَىٰ).
Added ي and ِ to show this is someone who helps frequently or intensely

steps.m: the one who helps a lot
steps.n: Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
Doubling the ن shows this is someone who helps in a very powerful or complete way
The ُ ending shows this word is describing Allah in this ayah
Added the vowels 'a' and 'i' to make it mean 'he knew' in the past
Changed from 'he knew' to 'know!' by adding alif at start and changing the middle vowel to sukoon - this makes it a command
Added وا to make it a command for multiple people
Added و at start to mean 'and', changed initial alif to hamzat wasl (ٱ), and added elongation mark on وا
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more certain

steps.m: that which/whatever (connects to غَنِمْتُم - you obtained as spoils)
steps.n: Adding ما changes the meaning from just 'that' to 'that which' or 'whatever'. This word introduces what was obtained as spoils of war (غَنِمْتُم).

steps.m: to gain something as a reward or prize

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this action

steps.m: you all gained (something as a prize)
steps.n: Changed from 'I' to 'you all' by adding م at the end
This is a basic word that shows where something comes from or that it's part of something bigger. Here it connects 'you obtained as spoils' (غَنِمْتُم) with 'anything' (شَىْءٍ).
Added a shadda (ّ) on the م to show it's pronounced more strongly because it comes after another similar sound.

steps.m: to want or be a thing

steps.n: The root letters combine to make the noun 'thing'. The hamza (ء) at the end is not needed for meaning - it's just part of how this common word is written.
Added the tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end because this word comes after the word مِن (from/of) which affects words that follow it. The ي is written as ى when it comes in the middle of a word.
This is a basic emphasis particle that shows certainty about what follows

steps.m: then surely (connects to غَنِمْتُم - you obtained as spoils)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement about obtaining spoils
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity or god
Added a double L sound (shadda) to change from 'any god' to 'Allah' - the One True God
Added لِ at beginning to show that something belongs to or is for Allah. Added kasra at end to connect it with what follows. This word shows that the fifth portion belongs to Allah.
The damma vowels make it into a noun meaning a fifth portion
The word changed to show it is the object being divided into fifths
Adds هُ to show this fifth belongs to Allah, with ۥ showing how to pronounce it longer
The و makes it mean 'someone who sends/delivers messages as their main job'
Added ال (the) at the start and a kasra at the end to show possession
Adding coordinating conjunction و

steps.m: and for the messenger (connected to لِلَّهِ - for Allah)
steps.n: Added لِ to show this belongs to or is for someone, connecting it with the earlier phrase لِلَّهِ (for Allah)
This is a special word used to show possession or ownership. Here it connects to 'the relatives' (ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ) in the next part of the ayah.

steps.m: for the owner of (connected to ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ)
steps.n: Added لِ to show this is being given or directed to someone, like saying 'for' or 'to' in English

steps.m: and for the owner of (connected to ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this phrase to the previous one about Allah's fifth share (لِلَّهِ خُمُسَهُۥ) and the messenger's share (لِلرَّسُولِ)
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the quality of being close
Added ى at the end to create a noun referring to people who have this quality
Added ال to make it specific and added the alif to extend the final ى

steps.m: to lose a parent/to be orphaned

steps.n: Changed from root meaning 'to be orphaned' into a noun meaning 'an orphan' by adding vowels
Changed to plural form by modifying internal vowels and adding ى at the end. This special plural pattern is used for words about people in difficult situations.

steps.m: the orphans (those who receive a share of war gains)
steps.n: Added ال to specify these particular orphans who are entitled to receive a portion of war gains according to this verse

steps.m: and the orphans (those who receive a share of war gains)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this group to the previous groups mentioned (Allah, the Messenger, and relatives) who all receive shares

steps.m: to be settled, to be at rest, to live in a place

steps.n: Forms noun of place from root meaning 'to dwell'
Makes the word plural by changing its internal structure
Makes a special plural form that often means 'people who are in need'
Adds kasra to indicate genitive case
Adds 'al' (ال) at the start to mean 'the' poor people instead of just poor people
Adds و meaning 'and' to connect this word to the previous items in the list of who gets a share

steps.m: to build or construct (also used for family relationships)

steps.n: The alif at the start shows this is telling someone to do something
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it, like saying 'son of'
Added و at beginning to connect this to previous items in the list. This word connects to السَّبِيلِ (the path/way) to form 'son of the path' meaning traveler.

steps.m: to make a path or way

steps.n: Adding ي and vowels creates the common noun meaning 'path'
Added ٱل to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda

steps.m: of the path (connected to ٱبْنِ meaning 'son/traveler of')
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it follows ٱبْنِ (son/traveler of) in the phrase ٱبْنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ (traveler of the path/wayfarer)
This is a conditional particle that connects two parts: 1) if something is true, 2) then something else happens. Here it connects to كُنتُمْ (you were) to start the condition.
Changed the root letters to make 'he was' by adding fathas (the small diagonal lines above)
Changed from past tense 'he was' to a command 'be!' by shortening the word and changing vowels
Added تُمْ at the end to show past tense for 'you all'
Changed from basic root meaning 'safety/trust' to Form IV verb meaning 'to believe' by adding long alif
Added تُم at the end to show that multiple people are being addressed
This is the unique proper name of God in Islam. The word comes from the root meaning of divinity but has become a specific name.
Added بِ at the beginning to show how something is done or connected to Allah, and added ِ at the end to connect it to the previous word.
Extended the alif at the end for emphasis and pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning, it just affects how the word is said.

steps.m: and what (connected to أَنزَلْنَا - we sent down)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what comes before it. This وَمَآ introduces what was sent down, connecting to the verb أَنزَلْنَا (we sent down) that comes right after it.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: to make descend, to send down
steps.n: Added 'أ' at start changes meaning from 'descended' to 'made something descend/sent down'
نا at the end shows that 'we' did the action. This is a past tense verb with 'we' as the subject.
This is a preposition that shows position or superiority. In this ayah, it connects to عَبْدِنَا (our servant) to show what was sent down upon.
Added an alif that looks like a little stick (ىٰ) at the end to show how to pronounce it correctly. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and change it from an action to a person who does that action
Added kasra at the end to show this word is owned by something
Added نَا at the end to show this servant belongs to us
Added vowels to the root letters to make it mean 'a day' or 'a specific time'

steps.m: on the day/at the time (of الْفُرْقَانِ - the criterion)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this word tells us when something happened. It's connected to the word الْفُرْقَانِ (the criterion) which comes after it.

steps.m: to separate or distinguish between things
clear distinction/criterion between right and wrong
steps.n: The ان ending makes the meaning more intense and complete
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific divine criterion rather than any criterion

steps.m: of the criterion (referring to divine guidance on the day of يَوْمَ)
steps.n: The kasra ending shows this word is connected to 'day' (يَوْمَ) in the ayah, meaning 'day of the criterion'
Basic meaning related to time and days
Forms the basic noun meaning 'day' by adding vowel marks. This is a common pattern for time-related nouns.

steps.m: on the day (of الْفُرْقَانِ - the criterion)
steps.n: Adds fatha at the end to show this word is describing when something happened. Here it's telling us when Allah sent down the criterion - on that specific day.
This root carries the basic meaning of meeting or encountering
Adding the vowel marks turns the root into a proper past tense verb meaning 'he met'
Changed to Form VIII (added اِلْتَ) to show two people meeting each other, like a two-way action
The اِ at the start is written as ٱ in the Quran

steps.m: a gathering/group (one of the two groups that met in battle)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a noun meaning 'group'. This word refers to one of the two armies that met at Badr.

steps.m: two groups (the two armies that met)
steps.n: Added ان to show there are exactly two groups being discussed - the Muslim army and their opponents

steps.m: the two groups (that met in battle)
steps.n: Added ال to specify these particular two groups mentioned in the battle context
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity
Added shadda on ل to make this word specific to Allah, the One True God
Added fatha under the shadda for proper pronunciation
Added damma at the end to show Allah is the subject (doer) in this sentence
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and', and added hamza with sukun as a helper for pronunciation
This is a preposition that shows something being sent down upon or revealed to someone. In this ayah, it shows that Allah sent down revelation upon His servant (Muhammad ﷺ).
The doubled ل with ُ on first letter makes the word mean 'all' or 'every'
The ِ at the end shows this word is connected to شَىْءٍ (thing) in the ayah, meaning 'of every thing'
Root letters ش-ي-أ got vowels (fatha and sukun) to make the noun 'thing'. The hamza (ء) is part of the root.
The ي is written as ى when it comes before hamza, but the pronunciation stays the same
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) because this word comes after مِن (from/of). When a word comes after مِن, it gets this ending to show it's being affected by the مِن.

steps.m: to determine, measure, or have power over

steps.n: Root letters connected with 'a' vowels to make a noun meaning power/ability
Added ي between second and third root letters to show intensity of quality

steps.m: All-Powerful (as a general attribute of اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added tanween dammah to show this is describing Allah's general attribute of being All-Powerful

steps.m: when (referring to the time of the battle of Badr)
steps.n: This is a particle that points to a specific time in the past. In this ayah, it refers to the time of the battle of Badr when the two armies were positioned on different sides.
Basic pronoun form for addressing someone
Adding م shows we're talking to multiple people (all male or mixed group)
This step changes the root from a verb meaning 'to be an enemy' to a noun meaning 'an enemy'.  It's like changing 'to run' to 'a runner'.
Adds vowels to make a noun meaning 'the edge/limit' of something, like a valley's bank
Makes it refer to a specific bank/edge that was known to the listeners

steps.m: at the bank/edge (near الدُّنْيَا - the nearer side)
steps.n: Shows location and connects to the word الدُّنْيَا (the nearer) which describes this bank in the ayah
These three letters together represent the basic idea of being near or close
Added special pattern to make it mean 'nearest' in a feminine way. The و changes to ي because it's easier to say.
Added ٱل at the beginning to say 'the nearest' instead of just 'nearest'. The د gets a shadda (ّ) because it comes after ٱل.
Added damma to show this is an independent pronoun meaning 'they' for a group of males or mixed gender
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English

steps.m: to go beyond bounds, move to the side

steps.n: Root meaning 'side' becomes a specific bank/edge by adding ة at the end
Makes it specific by adding the Arabic word for 'the'
Adds 'bi' (ب) at start to mean 'at/by' the bank

steps.m: by/at the bank/side (of الدُّنْيَا - the lower/nearer)
steps.n: Shows this bank/side belongs to or is described by الدُّنْيَا (the lower/nearer one)

steps.m: to be at the furthest point or edge

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he went far'
Changed from 'he went far' verb to 'furthest' by adding و and changing vowels, making it feminine with ى at the end
Added ال to make it specific
Added the long alif mark to show the extended sound. This word describes الْعُدْوَةِ (the bank/side) in the ayah.

steps.m: to mount, ride (especially a camel or horse)

steps.n: Changes from basic 'ride' meaning into a noun for 'riders traveling together'
Added definite article al-. The ل merges into ر creating a doubled sound (shown by ّ)

steps.m: and the group of mounted travelers
steps.n: Added conjunction wa- (and)

steps.m: and the group of mounted travelers (subject of أَسْفَلَ)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action. In this verse, this caravan is described as being أَسْفَلَ (below) the Muslims.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning and changed vowels to make it comparative, meaning 'lower than'. In this ayah, it describes the position of one group compared to another.
This is a preposition that shows something coming from or originating from somewhere
Added the pronoun كُمْ which means 'you all' to show who is being referred to
This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical scenario. In this ayah, it's used to discuss what would have happened if the Muslims had tried to plan the meeting at Badr themselves.
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before. This word introduces what would have happened if they had made an appointment to meet.

steps.m: to promise or set an appointment

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding alif changes it from a one-sided promise to a mutual promise between people

steps.m: they made mutual promises with each other
steps.n: Adding تَ at the beginning shows the action is reciprocal between multiple people

steps.m: you all made mutual promises with each other
steps.n: Adding تُمْ changes 'they' to 'you all' and shows it happened in the past

steps.m: to differ or come after something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added ٱ at start and ت after first letter to show people disagreeing with each other
Added لَ at beginning to show this is part of a 'what if' scenario
Added تُمْ at the end to show many people are being spoken to
Basic preposition showing location or containment. Here it connects with the word ٱلْمِيعٰدِ (the appointment) to show where/when something happens.

steps.m: to promise or set an appointment

steps.n: Changed from 'to promise' into 'appointment' by adding مِي at the start to make it mean 'place/time where something happens'. Like changing 'play' into 'playground'.
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ِ at the end to show it follows another word in the sentence
Combination of conjunction 'wa' (and) and adversative particle 'lakin' (but)
Added يَ at beginning to show who is doing the action (he) and when (present/future). The ْ (sukun) and ِ (kasra) help connect the letters properly.

steps.m: so that He (Allah) may decree (a matter that was مَفْعُولًا)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show purpose ('so that') and َ at end to show this is part of a purpose clause. This word connects to مَفْعُولًا (destined/decreed) later in the ayah.

steps.m: so that He may decree (with emphasis)
steps.n: Added shadda (ّ) on ل to emphasize the purpose
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Adding 'the' makes it refer to a specific deity
Shows this word is the subject of the sentence
Changed from basic root meaning 'command' into noun meaning 'matter' by adding marks (fatha-sukun pattern)

steps.m: a matter/affair (object of يَقْضِىَ - Allah decrees)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being decreed by Allah

steps.m: to be or exist in a state

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fathas) to change from basic root meaning 'to be' into 'he/it was'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in past tense
Shows this is something that Allah has done or decreed. The مَ at start and ول at end make it about receiving the action rather than doing it.
The ًا ending shows this describes أَمْرًا (a matter/affair) that was mentioned earlier
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added لِ (li) at the start to mean 'so that' or 'in order to', which changes the ending vowel
Added emphasis by doubling the ل with شدّة
This is a relative pronoun used for people. In this ayah, it refers to the person who 'lives' (حَىَّ), showing who is doing the action.

steps.m: he perished/died (subject of لِّيَهْلِكَ - so that he may perish)
steps.n: Basic verb pattern with fathas shows 'he' did the action in the past. This word appears in لِّيَهْلِكَ (so that he may perish) in the ayah.

This is a preposition that shows separation or moving away from something. In this ayah, it connects the verb حَىَّ (live/survive) with بَيِّنَةٍ (clear evidence), showing that the living/surviving happens based on or because of clear evidence.
Changed from verb 'to make clear' into adjective meaning 'clear' - like changing 'clarify' to 'clear'
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger
Adding ة makes it into a specific instance of clarity - a piece of evidence that makes something clear
Adding the two dots below (tanwin) changes it to mean 'with a clear proof' instead of just 'a clear proof'
Connected root letters with vowels and added shadda on middle letter since it's doubled
Added يَ at start to make it 'he' in present tense, and changed spelling at end because of special root letters
Added conjunction 'and' to connect with previous phrase
Added alif khanjariya (small alif) as pronunciation guide
This is a relative pronoun that refers to the person who is being destroyed. It's the subject of the verb هَلَكَ (perished/was destroyed) in this ayah.
Basic meaning of life/living
Added vowels to make past tense form. This word is connected to يَقْضِىَ (he decrees) in this ayah.
Changed the vowel after second letter from kasra to fatha to match regular past tense pattern

steps.m: from/away from (shows separation from بَيِّنَةٍ - clear evidence)
